div.head: The Special Representative of the President (Corrigan) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
12. The Commission at its meeting this morning decided to add two additional points to the list contained in my telegram No. 10 of November 12, 9 p.m., as follows: 1.
Both Governments offer to prevent fomentation in their territory and especially in frontier regions of revolutions against the other Government. 2.
Both Governments reaffirm that they will not resort to arms but will settle the dispute by the pacific means established by international law.

The Commission will meet again this afternoon to approve the wording of the various suggestions and plans to present them informally to the delegations tomorrow. They will be prepared and submitted to the two delegations for transmission to their Governments only after substantial agreement has been obtained.
